meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve no version control (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Idea Validation to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to master idea validation and overcome no version control. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first project.
content.answer_block: Stop flying blind. You solve 'no version control' by treating your idea validation as a codebase. Use Antigravity to structure your logic and Claude Code to iterate. By committing your validation steps as code, you create a permanent audit trail, ensuring your project evolves from a concept into a scalable reality.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank screen, a dozen half-baked features colliding in your head, and absolutely zero trail of how you arrived at your current mess. The 'no version control' trap is the graveyard of student projects. Without a history of your decisions, you are not building an app; you are drifting in a sea of forgotten code and abandoned logic.

But there is a way out. By treating your idea validation as a versioned engineering sprint, you transform chaos into a structured roadmap. We are going to use Antigravity to define your project architecture and Claude Code to maintain a rigorous, versioned history of your development milestones.

steps.title: Define the Core Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing code, define the single problem your app solves. This creates your project's 'source of truth'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Help me define the MVP scope for a student-focused project. Create a JSON schema that outlines the core problem, user persona, and top 3 features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create project_scope.json with defined MVP features'

steps.title: Initialize Versioned Architecture
steps.description: Set up a structured file system to ensure your logic is modular and trackable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a directory structure for a scalable web application. Include folders for /src, /docs, and /tests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize directory structure and create a README.md tracking the project inception'

steps.title: Automate Validation Logic
steps.description: Script your validation tests so that every feature addition is verified against your core value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite that checks if new features align with the core value proposition defined in project_scope.json.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create validation_test.py and run initial check'

steps.title: Implement Iterative Feedback Loops
steps.description: Use Claude Code to document every change, effectively creating a human-readable changelog for your validation process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a template for a development log that captures why a feature was added and how it solves a user pain point.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'append feature_details to CHANGELOG.md with timestamp and justification'

steps.title: Finalize and Deploy Validation Build
steps.description: Prepare your validated project for the world, ensuring the history of your development is clean and professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase against the initial MVP scope and suggest final cleanup tasks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate summary report of validation progress and prepare deployment configuration'

pro_tips: Treat your README.md as a live product roadmap—if it's not documented, it doesn't exist.
Use Claude Code's history logs as your primary documentation source; they are more accurate than memory.
Commit your validation logic as often as your code; it prevents feature creep by forcing you to justify every line.
Always run your validation test suite before pushing any changes to your main branch.
Build 'kill-switches' into your code early so you can disable experimental features without breaking the core app.
resources: Version Control Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your validation build is stable, push your repository to GitHub, document your learning journey on a personal portfolio site, and start seeking feedback from real users.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the roadmap to stop drifting and start shipping. Every line of code you write today is an asset for your future career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
